rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the target implementation scope when binding implementation-level signal flows or deployment mappings.
Preserve AADL architecture by ensuring annex content references only subcomponents, connections, and ports explicitly defined in the input model.
generic_rules: Scope-bound references must align with the hierarchical structure of the AADL model: type-level contracts for interfaces, implementation-level for bindings.
Only express relationships using elements that exist as named subcomponents or connections in the AADL architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and placed within implementation blocks for implementation-specific bindings.
Component ports referenced in annexes must be accessible through subcomponents or connections declared in the same scope.
anti_patterns: Do not inject references to AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not encode event ports as numeric or Boolean values without explicit data-value support in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
